channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: K9 Vajra-T to Get Advanced Anti-Drone Survival Kit with RWS, Jammers, and Akashteer Integration
item.description: The Indian Army’s K9 Vajra-T 155mm/52-caliber self-propelled howitzer is undergoing a major survivability upgrade, transforming it from a conventional artillery platform into a digitized combat system capable of defending itself against modern aerial threats. Operating close to the front lines and considered high-value assets, these guns are increasingly vulnerable to loitering munitions such as Harop and Lancet, as well as low-cost FPV suicide drones.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: GA-ASI Targets Indian Navy with Mojave STOL UAV for Carrier-Based Operations
item.description: General Atomics Aeronautical Systems Inc. (GA-ASI) is positioning its Mojave Short Takeoff and Landing (STOL) unmanned aerial system as a transformative solution for the Indian Navy’s future carrier-based and expeditionary aviation requirements. By combining runway independence with a proven tactical UAS architecture, the Mojave is being pitched as a force multiplier capable of redefining how unmanned systems are deployed in maritime combat environments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL Commits 10,000 Crore for Infrastructure Expansion to Boost Tejas and Helicopter Production
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) is set to undertake one of its most ambitious infrastructure development drives yet, with planned investments surpassing ?10,000 crore (roughly $1.1–1.2 billion). This substantial capital expenditure focuses squarely on scaling up manufacturing capacity to meet surging demand from the Indian Air Force and other armed services for indigenous platforms like the Tejas LCA Mk1A, advanced helicopters, and future programs.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Why the Indian Army Should Consider the HTT-40 as a Low-Cost Drone Hunter in the Era of Shahed-Style Threats
item.description: The character of aerial threats has shifted dramatically by 2026, with low-cost loitering munitions such as Shahed-class drones emerging as persistent and economically disruptive weapons. Lessons from Ukraine and the Middle East have exposed a fundamental mismatch between high-end air defense assets and these slow, expendable targets. In this evolving threat environment, turboprop aircraft are increasingly being recognized as a more efficient and tactically appropriate solution for counter-drone operations. Within this context, India’s indigenous HTT-40 basic trainer presents a compelling case for adaptation into a dedicated “drone hunter” platform.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Accelerates MUMT Warfare with CATS Warrior, FUFA and RSPA to Dominate Future Air Combat
item.description: India is taking bold strides into the future of aerial warfare by embracing Manned-Unmanned Teaming (MUM-T) concepts through a series of advanced unmanned stealth platforms, marking its first decisive move toward operationalizing loyal wingman and autonomous combat drones capable of challenging fifth-generation manned fighters while excelling in high-risk missions deep inside enemy territory.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Early MiG-21 Challenges for Indian Air Force: Lack of Air Conditioning and Operational Shortcomings During 1965 Indo-Pak War
item.description: In the mid-1960s, the Indian Air Force began inducting the Soviet-origin MiG-21, marking India’s entry into the supersonic fighter era. India had negotiated an agreement with the Soviet Union to acquire 12 MiG-21 aircraft along with setting up licensed production facilities domestically. By August 1965, these 12 jets had been delivered, yet they remained too new and unfamiliar for effective use during the 1965 Indo-Pakistani War.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Repositions Defence Attaché Network to Drive Arms Exports and Strategic Influence
item.description: India has undertaken a significant shift in its military diplomacy by restructuring its defence attaché network abroad, aligning it with a clear objective—boosting defence exports and expanding its footprint in the global arms market. This recalibration marks a transition from a traditionally import-oriented engagement model to one that actively promotes India as an emerging defence exporter.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF Chief Flies Boeing F-15EX that is on offer to India
item.description: Indian Air Force Chief Air Chief Marshal Amar Preet Singh (AP Singh) flew the F-15EX Eagle II fighter jet during his official visit to the United States on April 9, 2026. The familiarisation flight took place at Nellis Air Force Base in Nevada, where Singh flew in the backseat alongside US Air Force Major Matthew Benson of the 85th Test and Evaluation Squadron.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Russia, India Formalise Pact To Station Up To 3,000 Troops In Each Other’s Territory
item.description: Russia and India have agreed on a new format for military cooperation: the countries will be able to deploy aircraft, ships, and military personnel on each other’s territory. The corresponding intergovernmental agreement has already been published on the official legal information portal and entered into force on January 12.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Su-30MKI involved in the undercarriage failure at Pune International Airport has been successfully cleared
item.description: The Sukhoi Su-30MKI involved in the undercarriage failure at Pune International Airport has been successfully cleared, allowing flight operations to resume. The incident occurred around 10:25 PM on Friday, April 17, 2026, when the fighter jet experienced a hard landing.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: G20 satellite expected to be launched in 2027: ISRO chief Narayanan
item.description: ISRO Chairman V Narayanan said on Saturday (April 18, 2026) that the G20 satellite, designed to study climate, air pollution and monitor weather, is expected to be launched in 2027.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Puppet Label, Failed Leadership: Hafiz Saeed At Centre Of Lashkar-E-Taiba Rift
item.description: Signals of rifts within the Pakistan establishment relating to the revival of terror groups and leadership changes have emerged. It is a well known fact that Pakistan’s Field Marshal and Army Chief Asim Munir is unhappy with the manner in which the ISI has been functioning.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India attends France- and UK-led summit on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: India Friday joined a summit of dozens of countries that was hosted by France and the UK to deliberate securing the Strait of Hormuz for unimpeded shipping. New Delhi attended the summit as an observer. It is learnt that Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri represented India. Misri had participated in a similar meeting through virtual mode that was organised by the UK on April 2.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India showcases best practices in maritime security at Vienna conclave
item.description: India showcased its best practices in safeguarding maritime boundaries at a security congress in Vienna held from April 14-16, officials said on Friday. A three-member Indian delegation, led by a senior official, represented the country at the international forum. India showcased its leadership in maritime security and reinforced its commitment to international cooperation during its participation in the World Border Security Congress 2026 held in Vienna, Austria, the defence ministry said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: J&K: IGP Kashmir reviews security ahead of events?
item.description: Inspector General of Police, Kashmir Zone, V.K. Birdi, on Friday, chaired a security review meeting at the Police Control Room (PCR), Kashmir, to evaluate and finalise security arrangements for the forthcoming events in the Kashmir Valley.?
item.dc:creator: admin
